title: viola tricolor subsp. vulgaris
intro: Viola tricolor subsp. vulgaris, also known as heart's ease, is a flowering plant in the viola genus that is commonly found in Europe, Asia, and North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and acting as a natural antihistamine. Heart's ease can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, with the roots and flowers being the primary parts utilized.
